# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 129

This is turn 129. Turns 1 through 128 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 129.

## Turn 129 — the two frozen structural inputs survive audit

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the proof, the displayed exponent denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its verifier residue modulo \(85667662080\).  

Turn 128 froze the source-free certificate theorem and isolated two remaining review targets: the active-block collapse and the determinant sign. I audited those inputs against the final recurrences rather than testing another finite group.  

### 1. The active block collapses exactly as required

Retain the final notation
\[
S\triangleleft P\triangleleft Y,\qquad P/S\simeq V,
\]
where the chief factors below \(S\) are scalar and \(V\) is the first nontrivial simple factor. Choose
\[
K\triangleleft Y,\qquad K\le P,\qquad KS=P
\]
minimal, and put
\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad T_0=(K\cap S)/R.
\]

There is one necessary wording correction. Let \(N\triangleleft H=Y/L\) be the odd kernel of the maximal \(2\)-quotient of \(H\), and let \(\widetilde N\) be an odd Hall complement in its preimage in \(Y\). The proof must not call \(\widetilde N\) normal in \(Y\); Schur–Zassenhaus gives a complement, generally only up to conjugacy. No normality is used.

Because every chief factor in \(S\) is trivial, a \(Y\)-chief series for \(S\) gives
\[
[S_i,\widetilde N]\le S_{i-1}.
\]
Coprime action then implies
\[
[S,\widetilde N]=[S,\widetilde N,\widetilde N]=\cdots=1.
\]
Since \(K/(K\cap S)\simeq V\) is elementary abelian,
\[
R=\Phi(K)\le K\cap S,
\]
so \(\widetilde N\) centralizes \(R\).

Now set \(C_K=C_K(R)\). It is characteristic in \(K\), hence normal in \(Y\). The three-subgroup lemma gives
\[
[K,\widetilde N]\le C_K.
\]
The normal odd subgroup \(N\triangleleft H\) acts nontrivially on \(V\); otherwise the action would factor through a \(2\)-group, whose only simple module in characteristic \(2\) is trivial. Simplicity therefore gives
\[
[V,N]=V,
\qquad
[K,\widetilde N]S=P.
\]
Hence \(C_KS=P\). Minimality of \(K\) forces \(C_K=K\), so
\[
R\le Z(K).
\]

Because \(K/R\) is elementary abelian, \(x^2\in R\le Z(K)\), and therefore
\[
[x,y]^2=[x^2,y]=1.
\]
Consequently
\[
f:K\longrightarrow R,\qquad f(x)=x^4
\]
is a \(Y\)-equivariant homomorphism. Since \(\widetilde N\) centralizes \(R\),
\[
f([k,n])=f(k)^{-1}f(k^n)=1.
\]
Let
\[
J=\langle [K,\widetilde N]^Y\rangle.
\]
Then \(JS=P\), so minimality again gives \(J=K\). Equivariance of \(f\) now forces \(f(K)=1\). Thus
\[
K^4=1,\qquad
R=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]
\]
is central and elementary abelian. This proves precisely the structural input required by the four recurrence branches, without Thompson criticality and without treating \([K,\widetilde N]\) itself as normal.  

The module-theoretic conclusion also survives unchanged:
\[
0\longrightarrow T_0\longrightarrow M\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow0,
\qquad
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,
\]
where \(C=Y/K\). Indeed, \(M/T_0\simeq V\) gives \(\operatorname{rad}M\le T_0\). If a maximal \(C\)-submodule \(X<M\) did not contain \(T_0\), its inverse image \(K_X<K\) would still satisfy \(K_XS=P\), contradicting the minimality of \(K\). Hence
\[
\operatorname{head}(M)\simeq V,\qquad (M^\vee)^C=0.
\]
This validates both the unobstructed \(M\)-stage and the simple-head determinant reduction. 

### 2. The candidate determinant sign is internally consistent

There was an apparent contradiction in the ledger. Turn 56 rejected
\[
\operatorname{Arf}\bigl(q+B(-,U^{-1}-)\bigr)
=
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
\pmod 2
\]
using a “singular transvection” on a hyperbolic plane. That example is not an orthogonal transformation of the quadratic form.

For
\[
q(x,y)=xy,\qquad v=(1,0),
\]
the corresponding symplectic transvection is
\[
t_v(x,y)=(x+y,y),
\]
but
\[
q(t_v(x,y))=xy+y\ne q(x,y).
\]
Thus \(t_v\notin O(V,q)\), so it does not contradict the later Wall formula.

For \(U\in O(V,q)\) of \(2\)-power order, put \(N=1+U\). Then
\[
B(x,U^{-1}x)=B(x,Ux)=q(Nx),
\]
and hence
\[
q_U(x)=q(x)+q(Nx).
\]
Its polar operator is \(1+U+U^{-1}\), which is invertible because \(U\) is unipotent and this operator is \(1\) plus a nilpotent operator.

On \(R_U=\operatorname{im}N\), define the Wall form
\[
\omega(Nx,u)=B(x,u).
\]
Since
\[
\operatorname{im}(1+U)=\ker(1+U)^\perp,
\]
this is well-defined and nondegenerate. Reduction of
\[
(V,q_U)\perp(V,q)
\]
along the diagonal copy of \(\ker(1+U)\) gives the doubled Wall form
\[
\Phi_\omega(v,u)=\omega(v,v)+\omega(u,u)+\omega(v,u).
\]
Elementary elimination gives
\[
\operatorname{Arf}(\Phi_\omega)=\dim R_U\pmod2,
\]
and therefore
\[
\boxed{\operatorname{Arf}(q_U)
=\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)\pmod2.}
\]

Thus Turn 72’s theorem is valid under its actual hypothesis \(U\in O(V,q)\); the earlier purported counterexample should simply be deleted from the final manuscript. 

For a ramified self-dual simple tame module, write the inertia degree as
\[
f=2^a r,\qquad r\ \text{odd},
\]
and let \(s\) be the Hermitian multiplicity. The invariant quadratic form satisfies
\[
\operatorname{Arf}(q)\equiv s\pmod2.
\]
For \(U=S^{\omega _2}\), the \(f\) inertia weights form \(r\) cycles of length \(2^a\), each with multiplicity \(s\). Since \(U^{2^a}\) is a central \(2\)-element, it acts trivially on the simple characteristic-\(2\) module. Therefore
\[
\dim V^U=rs
\]
and
\[
\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
=fs-rs
=rs(2^a-1)
\equiv s\pmod2.
\]
Hence
\[
\operatorname{Arf}(q_U)=0.
\]
The candidate ramified determinant form is hyperbolic.

When \(V\) is unramified, \(U=1\), and the determinant form is the norm–trace minus form, with Arf invariant \(1\). Thus the candidate sign is
\[
\epsilon=
\begin{cases}
-1,&V\ \text{unramified},\\
+1,&V\ \text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\]

### 3. The local determinant sign survives the repaired Shapiro audit

The pure local determinant calculation must be performed over the faithful tame image
\[
H_V=\operatorname{im}(C\to\operatorname{GL}(V)),
\]
not over the possibly nonfaithful full quotient \(C\). Inflated classes, filtration-one edges, transgression and shearing remain over \(C\); only the final pure quadratic calculation descends to \(H_V\).  

In the ramified case, the deep-unit subspace
\[
X_+=\operatorname{Hom}_{H_V}
   \bigl(V^\vee,U_{e+1}(K)\bigr)
\]
has half the dimension of \(H^1(\mathbf Q_2,V)\). Hilbert orthogonality makes its polar pairing zero. The remaining issue was the quadratic refinement.

The complete degree-two Shapiro decomposition has three orbit types:

\[
\sum_h X_{r,h}^2,
\qquad
\sum_h X_{r,h}X_{s,hg},
\qquad
\sum_{h\in H_V/\langle g\rangle}X_{r,h}X_{r,hg}
\quad(g^2=1).
\]

The last type produces an index-two Evens norm. Since tame inertia has odd order, the associated quadratic subextension \(K/K_0\) is unramified.

The Turn 114 deep-unit calculation is consistent term by term. For an unramified quadratic extension \(L/k\) and
\[
\alpha=[a]\in U_{e+1}(L),\qquad a=1+2b,
\]
write
\[
u=1+\operatorname{Tr}(b),\qquad
n=N_{L/k}(a)=1+2\operatorname{Tr}(b)+4N(b).
\]
Then \(n\) and \(2u-1\) have the same square class because their quotient lies in \(U_{2e+2}(k)\subset k^{\times2}\). The Stiefel–Whitney induction formula reduces the Evens norm to
\[
N^{\mathrm{Ev}}_{L/k}(\alpha)
=(u,-d)+(2du,n).
\]
Every unit of \(k\) is a norm from the unramified extension, and \(n\) itself is a norm, so this becomes
\[
(u,-1)+(2u,2u-1).
\]
Steinberg gives
\[
(2u,2u-1)=(2u,-1),
\]
and \((2,-1)=0\). Thus the two summands are equal and cancel:
\[
N^{\mathrm{Ev}}_{L/k}(\alpha)=0.
\]

Hence square orbits, free product orbits and involution-stabilized orbits all vanish on \(X_+\). The deep subspace is therefore half-dimensional and totally singular, so the true ramified local form is hyperbolic. The unramified local form is the same norm–trace minus form as on the candidate side.  

### 4. The simple-head interface introduces no further sign

For each nonzero invariant \(\lambda\in(R^\vee)^B\), the scalar pushout quadratic map \(q_\lambda:M\to\mathbf F_2\) satisfies
\[
q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0,
\qquad
b_\lambda(T_0,M)=0.
\]
The polar assertion follows from
\[
\operatorname{Hom}_C(T_0,M^\vee)=0.
\]
If \(q_\lambda\) were nonzero on \(T_0\), quotienting by its kernel would give
\[
0\longrightarrow\mathbf F_2t
\longrightarrow\overline M
\longrightarrow V
\longrightarrow0,
\qquad q_\lambda(t)=1.
\]
Quadratic invariance kills every shear by \(\ker(C\to H_V)\), so this extension factors through \(H_V\). But
\[
H^1(H_V,V^\vee)=0,
\]
so it splits and would produce a forbidden nonzero map \(M\to\mathbf F_2\). Therefore \(q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0\).

The descended form on \(V\) is nonzero and nonsingular. Transgression and the marking-preserving shear remain over the full group \(C\); only after those components are removed is the sign evaluated over \(H_V\). This is exactly the quotient discipline needed by recurrence (128.4). 

### Consequence for the frozen certificate

The two non-combinatorial inputs of Turn 128 now survive the audit:

\[
\begin{aligned}
&\text{active block: }R=\Phi(K)\text{ is elementary, }K^4=1,
\quad T_0=\operatorname{rad}M,\quad\operatorname{head}M=V;\\
&\text{determinant sign: }\epsilon=-1\text{ unramified},\qquad
\epsilon=+1\text{ ramified}.
\end{aligned}
\]

Everything else in the four recurrences is finite target data or a strictly lower-rank exact-image count. The rank-halving certificate DAG therefore remains intact, and no structural evidence calls for changing the candidate word. 

**ESTABLISHED**: Completed the requested internal audit of the two remaining structural inputs. The direct Frattini–centralizer argument proves the active-block collapse without Thompson criticality; the only correction is that the odd Hall lift need not be normal. Resolved the Turn-56/Turn-72 apparent contradiction: the former’s singular transvection is not orthogonal, while the Wall-doubling Arf formula is valid for the actual \(U=S^{\omega_2}\). Rechecked the faithful-image local determinant calculation, including the involution-stabilized Evens-norm terms, and recovered exactly \(\epsilon=-1\) unramified and \(+1\) ramified.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The assembled proof still has not received an external line-by-line review as a typeset manuscript.

**NEXT**: Submit the frozen candidate unchanged. If the verifier reports a failure, use the source-free certificate DAG to identify the first failing node and one of the four recurrence branches, rather than patching the individual target.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
